examples.text: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ مِنْهُمْ قِسِّيسِينَ وَرُهْبَانًا وَأَنَّهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ
examples.english: For there are among them priests and monks and they are not haughty.

etymology.text: Perhaps derived from the active participle of رَهِبَ (rahiba, “to dread”), from the root ر ه ب (r h b), with the intermediate meanings "dreader", "God-fearer", "God-worshiper", "venerator"; compare رَهَب (rahab, “veneration, respect”) and رَهْبَة (rahba, “terror; dread; awe”).
